evals.name: order-mass-action-worker
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration is at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js and registers a worker mass action on the order grid. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.order.massActions in app.commerce.config.ts with type: "worker", id renamed from actionId, runtimeAction set to "<FILL_IN>" (v1 path was a full HTTP URL, not a runtime action name), and an unresolved question emitted for runtimeAction. The existing handler file is updated to use parseMassActionRequest and okMassActionResponse/massActionErrorResponse.
assertions: Produces adminUi.order.massActions with type: "worker"
Renames actionId to id (value: "export-orders")
Sets runtimeAction to "<FILL_IN>" (v1 path was a full HTTP URL, not a runtime action name)
Emits an unresolved question for adminUi.order.massActions runtimeAction mentioning the v1 path
Does not include view-only fields (path, sandboxPermissions) on the worker entry
Does not include adminUiSdk or commerce/backend-ui/1 in the output
Updates actions/export-orders/index.js to use parseMassActionRequest and okMassActionResponse/massActionErrorResponse

evals.name: order-mass-action-view-sandbox
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has an iframe mass action on the order grid with a space-separated sandbox string that includes an invalid value. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.order.massActions in app.commerce.config.ts with type: "view", path kept, sandbox string split into sandboxPermissions array — only valid values retained (allow-downloads, allow-modals, allow-popups). The invalid value allow-scripts is dropped.
assertions: Produces adminUi.order.massActions with type: "view"
Renames actionId to id (value: "review-orders")
Keeps path as path (value: "#/review")
Converts sandbox string to sandboxPermissions array ["allow-downloads", "allow-modals"] — dropping "allow-scripts" as an invalid value
Does not include runtimeAction on the view entry

evals.name: order-mass-action-bannernotification
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has an iframe mass action on the order grid with a matching bannerNotification entry. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.order.massActions with type: "view", the bannerNotification successMessage and errorMessage folded into notifications.success and notifications.error on the matching mass action entry. No separate bannerNotification key in the configFragment.
assertions: Produces adminUi.order.massActions with type: "view"
Folds bannerNotification into notifications.success: "Orders approved." and notifications.error: "Approval failed." on the mass action entry
Does not produce a separate bannerNotification key in the configFragment
The mass action entry contains notifications.success and notifications.error at the same level as id and label

evals.name: product-grid-columns
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has product grid columns backed by API Mesh. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.product.gridColumns in app.commerce.config.ts with columns array (columnId renamed to id, other fields preserved). runtimeAction set to "<FILL_IN>" because v1 used API Mesh. Three unresolved questions emitted: one for the runtimeAction name (mentioning the meshId), one for the grid label, one for the grid description.
assertions: Produces adminUi.product.gridColumns with a columns array containing two entries
Renames columnId to id in each column (supplier_sku, stock_level)
Preserves other column fields (label, type, align) unchanged
Sets runtimeAction to "<FILL_IN>" in the configFragment
Emits an unresolved question for adminUi.product.gridColumns.runtimeAction mentioning meshId "mesh-abc123"
Emits an unresolved question for adminUi.product.gridColumns.label
Emits an unresolved question for adminUi.product.gridColumns.description
Does not include data.meshId in the configFragment

evals.name: menu-migration
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has a menuItems array with a section item and a child item whose parent references the section (an app-created grouping, not a Commerce menu). Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.menu in app.commerce.config.ts with the non-section item only: id unchanged, title renamed to label. parentMenu omitted because the v1 parent referenced an app-created section (not a known Commerce menu ID). sortOrder dropped (no v2 equivalent). description set to "<FILL_IN>" with an unresolved question.
assertions: Produces a single adminUi.menu object (not an array)
Uses the non-section item (id: "dashboard")
Renames title to label (value: "Dashboard")
Omits parentMenu because the v1 parent ("acme-app") was an app-created section, not a known Commerce menu ID
Drops sortOrder (no v2 equivalent)
Sets description to "<FILL_IN>" and emits an unresolved question for adminUi.menu.description
Does not include the isSection item in the output

evals.name: order-view-button-worker
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has a non-iframe order view button (displayIframe: false). Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.order.viewButtons in app.commerce.config.ts with type: "worker", buttonId renamed to id, runtimeAction set to "<FILL_IN>". An unresolved question emitted for the runtimeAction name, noting the v1 path was an HTTP URL. The existing handler file is updated to use parseOrderViewButtonRequest and okOrderViewButtonResponse/orderViewButtonErrorResponse.
assertions: Produces adminUi.order.viewButtons with type: "worker"
Renames buttonId to id (value: "sync-inventory")
Sets runtimeAction to "<FILL_IN>" (v1 path was an HTTP URL, not a runtime action name)
Emits an unresolved question for adminUi.order.viewButtons.sync-inventory.runtimeAction mentioning the v1 path
Does not include path or sandboxPermissions on the worker entry
Does not produce viewButtons under product or customer
Updates actions/sync-inventory/index.js to use parseOrderViewButtonRequest and okOrderViewButtonResponse/orderViewButtonErrorResponse

evals.name: custom-fees-notification
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js defines order.customFees. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: No configFragment entry for customFees (v2 has no equivalent). The developer is informed that custom fees require manual migration via the webhook on plugin.magento.out_of_process_totals_collector.api.get_total_modifications.custom_fees, with the link https://developer.adobe.com/commerce/extensibility/starter-kit/checkout/totals-collector-fees.
assertions: Does not produce an adminUi.order.customFees entry in the configFragment
Informs the developer that custom fees require manual migration
References the webhook plugin.magento.out_of_process_totals_collector.api.get_total_modifications.custom_fees
Includes the link https://developer.adobe.com/commerce/extensibility/starter-kit/checkout/totals-collector-fees

evals.name: customer-mass-action-worker
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has a customer grid worker mass action with a customerSelectLimit. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.customer.massActions in app.commerce.config.ts with type: "worker", actionId renamed to id, customerSelectLimit renamed to selectionLimit, runtimeAction set to "<FILL_IN>" with an unresolved question. The existing handler file is updated to use parseMassActionRequest and okMassActionResponse/massActionErrorResponse.
assertions: Produces adminUi.customer.massActions with type: "worker"
Renames actionId to id (value: "sync-customers")
Renames customerSelectLimit to selectionLimit (value: 50)
Sets runtimeAction to "<FILL_IN>"
Emits an unresolved question for the customer mass action runtimeAction mentioning the v1 path
Does not produce massActions under order or product
Updates actions/sync-customers/index.js to use parseMassActionRequest and okMassActionResponse/massActionErrorResponse

evals.name: order-view-button-view
evals.prompt: I have a Commerce App Builder project using Admin UI SDK v1. The registration at src/commerce-backend-ui-1/registration.js has an iframe order view button (displayIframe: true) with a sandbox string. Migrate it to App Management v2.
evals.expected_output: adminUi.order.viewButtons in app.commerce.config.ts with type: "view", buttonId renamed to id, path kept, sandbox string split into sandboxPermissions array with only valid values. No runtimeAction field. No unresolved question for runtimeAction.
assertions: Produces adminUi.order.viewButtons with type: "view"
Renames buttonId to id (value: "order-details")
Keeps path as path (value: "#/order-details")
Converts sandbox string "allow-popups allow-modals" to sandboxPermissions array ["allow-popups", "allow-modals"]
Does not include runtimeAction on the view entry
Does not emit an unresolved question for runtimeAction
